referenced_message.author: contracept
referenced_message.content: 5 seconds isnt long enough

content: 5 seconds isnt long enough

Adjustment: Add a strict internal cooldown to the crowd-control aspect of the talent in PvP.
Impact: Add a modifier stating: "The Stun effect can only be applied to the same enemy player once every 12–15 seconds." This completely breaks the infinite stunlock and gives the opposing player a window to react.

¿The Core Problem: Why It’s Broken?

Shotgun Multi-Hit Abuse (Infinite Stun):
The talent states: "While possessing Extra Health, apply Stun after every 10 hits performed." Shotguns fire multiple pellets per shell. Currently, the game registers every individual pellet as a distinct "hit." 
A single close-range shotgun blast can immediately register  8 hits, meaning just two quick shots will instantly and repeatedly trigger the Stun effect.

The "Extra Health" Loop:
To activate the stun, the player needs Extra Health. By pairing this protocol with weapons that generate shields on hit and talents that grant shields on reload, shotgun players maintain a permanent flow of overheal/shields. This keeps the protocol’s condition active 100% of the time.

Lack of Diminishing Returns / Internal Cooldown:
While the card shows a "Talent Cooldown: 10 seconds" at the bottom, this only seems to apply to the +2400% Firepower buff. 

The actual Stun effect has no internal cooldown per target, resulting in a total stunlock where the victim cannot move, shoot, or counterplay until they are dead.

Yes, I believe Pummeling Shield needs changes, but I would approach it through a broader crowd-control system rather than targeting a single OS.
The biggest issue is not necessarily the initial stun itself, but the ability for players to be repeatedly stunned with little or no opportunity to respond. In PvP, this can create situations where a player is effectively locked down until they are eliminated.
To address this, I believe all stun effects should be subject to diminishing returns.
For example:
First stun: Full duration.
Second stun: Reduced duration.
Third stun: Further reduced duration.
Fourth stun attempt: Target becomes immune to stun effects for 5 seconds.
After the immunity period ends, the cycle resets.
This system would apply to all stun effects in the game, including Pummeling Shield, Bulwark, and any future abilities or gear that introduce crowd-control mechanics.
In addition, I believe the Bulwark stun duration should be reduced to approximately 2 seconds when applied to players in PvP content.
The goal is not to remove crowd control from the game. Stuns should remain useful and impactful. However, players should always have an opportunity to recover and respond rather than being permanently controlled until death.
Implementing diminishing returns on crowd-control effects would create healthier PvP encounters, reduce frustration, and prevent future crowd-control abilities from creating the same problems. It would also encourage skillful timing of stuns rather than simply chaining them repeatedly.
Overall, I believe a universal crowd-control diminishing returns system would be a better long-term solution than repeatedly nerfing individual abilities whenever they become problematic.

referenced_message.author: daviscrimson
referenced_message.content:
Main Issue : OS Protocol Pummeling Shield

The OS Protocol Pummeling Shield is considered "broken" and "imbalanced" because it allows for unlimited stuns against other players as long as the user maintains full or extra health.

Proposed Solutions:

Cooldown : Implement a minimum 6-second cooldown on the stun effect.

Duration : Reduce the stun duration to a maximum of 2 seconds.

Objective : The goal of these changes is to ensure a more enjoyable and fair experience for both agents and rogue agents within the Dark Zone.

Duration : Reduce the stun duration to a maximum of 2 seconds.

Objective : The goal of these changes is to ensure a more enjoyable and fair experience for both agents and rogue agents within the Dark Zone.

content:
Yes, I believe Pummeling Shield needs changes, but I would approach it through a broader crowd-control system rather than targeting a single OS.
The biggest issue is not necessarily the initial stun itself, but the ability for players to be repeatedly stunned with little or no opportunity to respond. In PvP, this can create situations where a player is effectively locked down until they are eliminated.
To address this, I believe all stun effects should be subject to diminishing returns.
For example:
First stun: Full duration.
Second stun: Reduced duration.
Third stun: Further reduced duration.
Fourth stun attempt: Target becomes immune to stun effects for 5 seconds.
After the immunity period ends, the cycle resets.
This system would apply to all stun effects in the game, including Pummeling Shield, Bulwark, and any future abilities or gear that introduce crowd-control mechanics.
In addition, I believe the Bulwark stun duration should be reduced to approximately 2 seconds when applied to players in PvP content.
The goal is not to remove crowd control from the game. Stuns should remain useful and impactful. However, players should always have an opportunity to recover and respond rather than being permanently controlled until death.
Implementing diminishing returns on crowd-control effects would create healthier PvP encounters, reduce frustration, and prevent future crowd-control abilities from creating the same problems. It would also encourage skillful timing of stuns rather than simply chaining them repeatedly.
Overall, I believe a universal crowd-control diminishing returns system would be a better long-term solution than repeatedly nerfing individual abilities whenever they become problematic.
